## Summary

<!-- What does this PR change and why? Link to issues where relevant. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing behavior to change)
- [ ] Documentation only
- [ ] Refactor / chore (no user-visible behavior change)
- [ ] Test coverage only

## Related issue

<!-- e.g. Fixes #123, Relates to #456 -->

## How tested

<!-- Paste commands run and summarized results. Evidence before claims. -->

```bash
# Example:
npm run build
npm test
# npm run test:e2e:smoke   # if stack-dependent
```

**Results:**

<!-- exit codes, pass/fail counts, or relevant output snippets -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests added or updated for behavior changes (or N/A with reason)
- [ ] Documentation updated (`README.md`, `docs/`, etc.) when setup or behavior changed
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` updated under `[Unreleased]` when user-facing (or N/A)
- [ ] I read and followed [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] PR is focused on a single logical change (unrelated cleanups are in separate PRs)
- [ ] No secrets, credentials, or `.env` values committed
